headline: Nomura: Hyperscaler spending and bottlenecks will keep chip rally alive
detail: Nomura's call directly supports your biggest sector bet - SMH, SOXL, CRWD, and any chip-adjacent names. The thesis is that forced capex spending by hyperscalers creates persistent demand, meaning the rally isn't just momentum but has a fundamental floor. This is the single most relevant headline for your book today.
rec: You're already long SMH and SOXL - this validates holding both. If you want to add chip exposure, AMAT has a scanner setup (9/12 grade A... wait, AMAT is 7/12) at entry 721.55-722.28, stop 720.47; only consider it if price pulls to that zone cleanly. No need to chase - you're already in the right seats.

headline: Tech leads first-half stock gains, biggest winners outside U.S.
detail: Confirms the tech-heavy positioning you already have is on the right side of the dominant first-half theme. However, the note that biggest winners were non-U.S. is worth watching - if international tech ETFs start pulling capital away from U.S. names, that could pressure QQQ and SMH at the margin. For now it's a hold-your-ground signal.
rec: No action needed - your QQQ (+3.04%) and SMH (+3.07%) are participating fine. Just note that if non-U.S. tech momentum accelerates, it could dilute flows into your names; nothing to trade off today.

headline: UK defense stocks surge on $20B spending boost
detail: Interesting for global defense broadly, but you have zero direct defense exposure. RTX shows up on the scanner (7/12, Grade A) and might benefit from a spillover defense-spending narrative, but it's not your sector and there's no catalyst specific to U.S. defense from this headline alone.
rec: RTX is on the scanner but defense isn't your book and this is a UK-specific catalyst. Not a reason to rotate out of tech/chips - skip it.

headline: Oil wobbles on U.S.-Iran uncertainty
detail: Oil uncertainty tends to create mild risk-off noise that can briefly pressure the broader market, including tech. ERY (inverse energy 3x ETF) is on the scanner as a Grade A setup, which would benefit if oil drops further - but this is a speculative short-energy trade with no direct relevance to your core chip/tech thesis.
rec: ERY is on the scanner but adding a leveraged inverse energy ETF on top of your already leveraged, margin-heavy tech book adds unrelated risk. Oil noise isn't a reason to act - hold your lane.
